full_text:
FACS Staining Protocol_immune cell profiling

Antibody Panel

Surface Staining Protocol

Aliquot up to 3 x 106 cells into an Eppendorf tube, add 900 µL FACS staining buffer (2% FBS, 2mM EDTA in PBS) and centrifuge (6 min, +4°C, 400g).

Meanwhile, prepare the antibody master mix for the surface antigens:

For each sample you should prepare 50 µL of antibody master mix

This mix contains all the antibodies in the panel at a specific titration to maximize the distance between the positive and negative populations

Add the remaining volume (to make up to 50 µL) of FACS staining buffer – it is a good idea to add this to an Eppendorf before adding the master mix.

NOTE! Dead cell marker (DCM) is not an antibody and can be detrimental to the other antibodies in the master mix if stored together for too long. Thus, add the DCM to the antibody master mix just before adding the mix to the cell pellet.

After the centrifugation, discard the supernatant (by pipetting) leaving the cell pellet intact. Resuspend the pellet into 50 µL antibody master by gently pipetting up and down. Incubate for 30 min, +4°C in dark.

Wash: after the incubation, add 900 µl FACS staining buffer and centrifuge (6 min, +4°C, 400g). Discard the supernatant.

Repeat the wash (STEP 4)

Leaving approximately 50 µL, discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet. Resuspend the pellet into the residual fluid by vortexing at low speed.

If the cells are run the next day, fix them with BD CellFIX -> dilute 10X BD CellFIX (room-temperature, 20°C – 25°C) to 1X with MQ water. Add 0.5 mL of 1X BD CellFIX solution to each tube except. Vortex thoroughly at low speed for 3 seconds. Make sure

Store samples in +4°C, dark. Run the samples within 24 hr after the fixation.

If the cells are analysed on the same day, resuspend the pellet from step 7 into 250 µL of FACS staining buffer. Filter the suspension into the FACS tube through a filter top to remove debris and cell clumps.

Run the cells on Fortessa.

Export the data to a hard drive.

Analyse the samples using FlowJo.

Protocol for Single Colour Compensations

Single cell compensations should be run when using or setting up a new panel -> and then around every month if you continue to use the panel for subsequent samples.

Add the positive compensation beads to each FACS tube for every fluorochrome in the panel + one extra which will be your unstained control -> you will need to add the negative beads to this tube too

Note that the beads you are using have to correspond to the type of antibody you are using -> most are mouse derived but some are from rats which require different beads.

Add 1 µL of a single fluorochrome conjugated antibody to the corresponding labelled FACS tube -> incubate 30 mins, +4°C in dark and add 250 µL of FACS Staining buffer before running.

When Setting up a panel for the first time, these compensations should be used to determine the PMT voltages for each fluorochrome

Fluorochrome | Antibody | Marker for | Host species | Dilution | Volume (µL) per 100 µL
FITC | DCM | Dead cells | | 1:200 | 0.5
PE | CD14 | Macrophages | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
PerCP-CY5.5 | HLA-DR | APC | Mouse | 1:25 | 4
PE-CY7 | CD8 | Cytotox T-cells | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
APC | CD4 | Helper T-cells | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
AF700 | CD56 | NK cells | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
APC-CY7 | EpCam (CD326) | Tumor / epithelial | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
BV421 | CD45 | Leukocytes | Mouse | 1:200 | 0.5
BV510 | CD16 | Neutrophils, Monocytes, Macrophages, NK cells | Mouse | 1:200 | 0.5
BV605 | CD20/CD19 | B cells | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
BV711 | CD11c | Dendritic, Monocytes, Macrop. | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
BV786 | CD3 | T cells | Mouse | 1:50 | 2
SUM | SUM | SUM | SUM | SUM | 21.5
Volume of the buffer | Volume of the buffer | Volume of the buffer | Volume of the buffer | Volume of the buffer | 78.5
